All briefs
Mar 05, 2026 · 4 min read

AI Brief — March 5, 2026

LLMEnterprise AIRegulationChinese AI

Items from the past 7 days only. Sources linked inline.

Model Releases & Updates

OpenAI — GPT-5.3 Instant launched — Available to all ChatGPT users and developers via API as gpt-5.3-chat-latest. Delivers improved tone, relevance, and conversational flow with better web search. GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and o4-mini retired from ChatGPT. GPT-5.2 Instant moves to Legacy Models, retiring June 3. (OpenAI News · Releasebot)

OpenAI — Codex app for Windows — Now available for ChatGPT Business workspaces, enabling multiple parallel Codex agents with isolated worktrees and reviewable diffs. (Releasebot)

Anthropic — Claude Code & Opus 4.6 updates — Opus 4.6 now defaults to medium effort for Max and Team subscribers. Claude Code received updates including API skill, session naming, keypad support, multi-language voice STT, and agent/worksphere UI improvements. (Releasebot)

Alibaba — Qwen 3.5 Small models released — Four new models (0.8B, 2B, 4B, 9B parameters) designed for on-device AI and lightweight agents. Alibaba also unified all large model branding under "Qwen." (MarkTechPost)

DeepSeek — V4 launch expected imminently — Trillion-parameter multimodal system (text, image, video, audio) optimized for coding and long-context tasks. Developed with Huawei/Cambricon chips; planned open-source release. (AI Haberleri)

⚠️ Action item — OpenAI container billing: Starting March 31, 2026, container usage billed per 20-minute session. (OpenAI Pricing)

Chinese AI Labs (Qwen / Kimi / DeepSeek)

Qwen leadership shakeup — Junyang Lin, tech lead for Alibaba's Qwen project, stepped down March 4. Alibaba formed a new task force to accelerate foundation model development. Community member described departure as "the end of an era." (TechCrunch · VentureBeat)

Moonshot AI / Kimi K2.5 revenue milestone — K2.5 generated more revenue in its first 20 days than Moonshot earned in all of 2025. Valuation doubled to $10B+ (fastest Chinese AI decacorn). Two funding rounds totaling $1.2B closed within 3 months (Alibaba, Tencent). Agent Swarm beta can direct up to 100 sub-agents. (HelloChinaTech · InfoQ)

Distillation controversy escalates — Anthropic accused DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax of "industrial-scale" distillation via ~24,000 fraudulent accounts generating 16M+ exchanges with Claude. OpenAI made similar allegations to the US House Select Committee on China. (PYMNTS)

Agentic Systems & Frameworks

Microsoft merges AutoGen + Semantic Kernel — AutoGen moves to maintenance mode as Microsoft consolidates into a unified "Microsoft Agent Framework" SDK, 1.0 GA targeted Q1 2026. (CalmOps)

LangGraph v1.0 — default for LangChain agents — Now the production runtime for all LangChain agents, dominant for complex enterprise orchestration with 40%+ performance improvements via parallel execution. (SoftmaxData)

CrewAI adds A2A protocol support — Expanding agent interoperability via Agent-to-Agent protocol while maintaining role-based collaboration patterns. (OpenAgents)

Workflow Automation

Zapier — Agents & Copilot — Launched autonomous AI teammates for multi-step tasks across 8,000+ apps, plus Copilot for natural-language workflow building. (Zapier Blog)

n8n — 70+ AI-native nodes — Ships AI-specific nodes spanning LLMs, embeddings, vector DBs, OCR, and image gen. Positioned as serious enterprise alternative after $55M Series B. (n8n Blog)

Make — AI scenarios — Introduced built-in prompt engineering interfaces for AI-powered automation. (Digidop)

Pricing & API Changes

| Model / Service | Change | Effective |

|---|---|---|

| OpenAI GPT-5.3 Instant | Replaces GPT-5.2 Instant as default | Now |

| OpenAI containers | Billed per 20-min session | March 31, 2026 |

| Global AI opex | Exceeds $500B in 2026 (up 300% from 2024) | Ongoing |

AI cost crisis — 70% of CIOs in a Forrester survey cite "AI cost unpredictability" as top barrier to adoption. API aggregation platforms emerging to slash integration costs by up to 80%. (EIN Presswire)

Enterprise AI News

OpenAI closes $110B funding — Led by Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank. Valued at $840B post-money. Includes $100B AWS compute agreement. 900M weekly active users. (AWS Blog)

Anthropic nears $20B revenue run rate — Up from $9B at end of 2025, driven by Claude models and Claude Code adoption. (Bloomberg)

Anthropic–Pentagon dispute — Dario Amodei reopened talks with Pentagon after safety feud. Pentagon designated Anthropic as supply-chain risk. Amodei called OpenAI's military deal messaging "straight up lies." (TechCrunch · CNBC)

Meta tests AI shopping — New chatbot feature shows product carousels with pricing, rivaling ChatGPT/Gemini shopping. Also announced long-term AMD deal for up to 6GW of Instinct GPUs. (Bloomberg)

Mistral AI — finance services — New offering lets financial firms keep data in-house. Signed 3-year strategic partnership with Tesco. (Bloomberg)

Google absorbs Intrinsic for robotics — DeepMind AI + Intrinsic manufacturing automation. Pichai compared platform to "Android for robotics." Also launched European robotics startup accelerator. (ResultSense)

xAI regulatory pressure — UK's ICO/Ofcom and California AG issued formal demands to stop Grok from producing non-consensual deepfake content. (LLM Stats)
